# Feature: MVE dialog clip dedup (single textbox per lane)

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-12 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent

Prevent two `MveDialogClipCard` instances from rendering in the same audio lane when an orphan dialog clip at `startSec≈0` coexists with an unbound text-only MVE line for the same character and scene. Bind the existing text-only line to the clip instead of creating a duplicate line, and skip the audio-bound MVE segment when a conflicting text-only sibling still exists.

## Preconditions

- Desktop local runtime with an open `.scriptony` Hörspiel project (MVE enabled)
- Character dialog lane with at least one text-only MVE line (no `audioClipId`)
- Orphan dialog/narrator clip in the same lane and scene (e.g. `startSec≈0`) without a bound MVE line

## Happy Path

- [ ] Lane shows exactly one inline MVE dialog card for the character+scene pair (not two stacked textboxes)
- [ ] Backfill / `ensureForClip` binds the existing text-only line to the orphan clip instead of creating a second line
- [ ] After bind, the line disappears from the text-only lane items list and renders via the audio clip segment (or remains as text-only if clip has no waveform yet)

## Edge Cases

- [ ] Clip already has a bound line: no second line created; no duplicate card
- [ ] Multiple unbound lines in same scene for same character: lowest `orderIndex` line is bound first
- [ ] SFX/music clips: unchanged (no MVE line backfill)
- [ ] Pre-existing duplicate lines in DB: render dedup hides the clip-bound MVE segment while an unbound text-only sibling exists

## Regression

- [ ] Text-only MVE blocks without orphan clips still render via `MveTextBlockLaneItems`
- [ ] Audio-bound clips with no unbound sibling still render `AudioTimelineMveDialogSegment`
- [ ] Cross-scene move and in-scene reorder unchanged
- [ ] Generate/upload/record audio from text-only block still binds clip correctly

## Assumptions

- One text-only line per character+scene should bind to at most one orphan clip; additional clips in the same scene get new lines
- Render dedup is a safety net for legacy duplicate data; primary fix is bind-in-`ensureMveLineForClip`

## Implementation Notes

- Files touched: `src/lib/mve/mve-dialog-clip-dedup.ts`, `ensure-mve-line-for-clip.ts`, `AudioClipLaneContent.tsx`
- Unit tests: `mve-dialog-clip-dedup.test.ts`, extended `ensure-mve-line-for-clip.test.ts`
- Backfill uses existing `ensureForClip` → bind path; render dedup is safety net for legacy duplicates
